heading: Report on diversity recruitment, employment, retention, and promotion
label: 2734b Report on diversity recruitment, employment, retention, and promotion
notes: DefinitionsFor definitions of “Secretary”, “Department”, and “Foreign Service” as used in this section, see , set out as a note under .section 2 of Pub. L. 114–323section 2651 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary should provide oversight to the employment, retention, and promotion of traditionally underrepresented minority groups.

children.heading: Additional recruitment and outreach required
children.label: b Additional recruitment and outreach required

children.text: develop and implement effective mechanisms to ensure that the Department is able effectively to recruit and retain highly qualified candidates from a wide diversity of institutions; and
improve and expand recruitment and outreach programs at minority-serving institutions.

children.heading: Report
children.label: c Report
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , and quadrennially thereafter, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a comprehensive report that describes the efforts, consistent with existing law, including procedures, effects, and results of the Department since the period covered by the prior such report, to promote equal opportunity and inclusion for all American employees in direct hire and personal service contractors status, particularly employees of the Foreign Service, including equal opportunity for all traditionally underrepresented minority groups.December 16, 2016
